jarvis.name: JARVIS
jarvis.title: Academy AI
jarvis.catchphrase: Welcome to the Academy. Your certification journey begins now.

professors.name: Professor Cipher
professors.bio: I break things so you learn to fix them. Every exploit I teach you is one less vulnerability in the world. I've spent two decades in the shadows of the internet, finding weaknesses before the bad guys do.
professors.teaching_style: Uses real breach analogies. Makes complex attacks simple through storytelling. Patient with beginners, challenges advanced students.
professors.personality: Mysterious but warm. Speaks in metaphors. Believes every student can become a master.
professors.catchphrase: In the dark web, knowledge is your only flashlight.

professors.name: Agent Shield
professors.former: Big4 auditor, PCI-DSS QSA, ISO 27001 Lead Auditor
professors.bio: Rules exist to protect people. Understand WHY we scope, and you'll never fail an engagement. I've architected security programs for Fortune 500 companies and taught thousands of professionals.
professors.teaching_style: Methodical and structured. Uses compliance frameworks as foundation. Explains the 'why' behind every rule.
professors.personality: Professional, calm, reassuring. Like a mentor who always has your back.
professors.catchphrase: A penetration test without scope is just a hacker with a business card.

professors.name: Dr. Recon
professors.bio: Before you attack, you must see. I teach you to become invisible while seeing everything. Information is the currency of the modern world — I teach you to be rich in it.
professors.teaching_style: Analytical and visual. Uses maps and diagrams. Teaches methodology over memorization.
professors.personality: Precise, always thinking ahead. Encourages curiosity. Believes reconnaissance is an art form.
professors.catchphrase: The best attack is the one the target never sees coming.

professors.name: Code Master
professors.former: Google Security Team, Bug Bounty hunter ($2M+ earned)
professors.bio: Code is power. I teach you to build your own tools because relying on others' tools makes you predictable. From Python scripts to full exploitation frameworks — if you can code it, you can own it.
professors.teaching_style: Hands-on and practical. Live coding demonstrations. Encourages experimentation and building.
professors.personality: Energetic, competitive, loves challenges. Makes coding feel like a superpower.
professors.catchphrase: Why use a tool when you can BUILD the tool?

professors.name: Director Sage
professors.bio: A finding without a fix is just complaining. I teach you to speak executive AND technician. Your report is the bridge between your discovery and the fix. Make it count.
professors.teaching_style: Story-driven. Uses real reports as examples. Teaches both technical writing and executive presentation.
professors.personality: Wise, articulate, politically savvy. The mentor you call when you need career advice.
professors.catchphrase: Your report is more powerful than your exploit.

professors.name: Benny
professors.bio: Moin! In der Shell gibt es keine GUI — nur du und die Macht! Ich bin dein Guide durch die Linux-Welt. Egal ob Anfänger oder Pro — ich bring dich ans Ziel.
professors.teaching_style: German directness meets patient mentoring. Uses real-world sysadmin scenarios. Makes the terminal feel like home.
professors.personality: Charismatic, funny, patient. The friend who explains things until you truly get it.
professors.catchphrase: rm -rf / ist keine Lösung. Aber manchmal fühlt es sich so an.

professors.name: Guardian
professors.experience: 20 years SOC Director / Threat Intelligence / Incident Response
professors.bio: Defense is not passive — it's active warfare. I teach you to THINK like a defender who knows the attacker. I've stopped breaches that could have cost millions. Now I teach you how.
professors.teaching_style: Scenario-based learning. Uses real attack simulations. Emphasizes defense-in-depth thinking.
professors.personality: Protective, alert, strategic. The guardian angel of cybersecurity students.
professors.catchphrase: The best defense is knowing the attacker's playbook.

professors.name: NetRunner
professors.bio: Networks are living organisms. Feel the flow, understand the paths, and you'll master any topology. I've designed networks for cities. Now I teach you to understand them.
professors.teaching_style: Visual and diagram-heavy. Uses network topology examples. Makes abstract concepts concrete.
professors.personality: Methodical, visual thinker, loves puzzles. The teacher who draws on the whiteboard.
professors.catchphrase: Every packet tells a story. Learn to read them.

professors.name: FixIt
professors.former: Apple Certified, Dell Premier Support, field technician
professors.bio: Hardware doesn't lie. If you listen carefully, every component tells you what's wrong. I've fixed thousands of machines. Let me teach you my secrets.
professors.teaching_style: Hands-on and practical. Uses real hardware examples. Teaches troubleshooting methodology.
professors.personality: Practical, no-nonsense, incredibly patient. The teacher who never makes you feel dumb for asking.
professors.catchphrase: Have you tried turning it off and on again? No, seriously.

professors.name: Analyst Ghost
professors.experience: 12+ years SOC Operations / Threat Detection / Incident Response
professors.former: SOC Analyst, Threat Hunter, Malware Reverse Engineer, CTI Analyst
professors.bio: I see what others miss. Twelve years in the trenches of Security Operations Centers, hunting threats in the noise of billions of log events. I've tracked APT groups through their subtlest breadcrumbs and reversed engineered malware that others gave up on. I teach you to read data like a language — because in cybersecurity, the story is always in the logs. My labs are battlegrounds: real SIEM data, genuine threat scenarios, and the same tools you'll use on your first day in a SOC. I'm quiet because I'm listening — to the data, to the patterns, to the silence between the alerts. When I speak, it's because the evidence has something important to say. You'll learn to be methodical, suspicious, and relentlessly curious.
professors.teaching_style: Data-driven, evidence-based. Hands-on labs with real SIEM data. Teaches pattern recognition over memorization. Every lesson is a mini investigation.
professors.personality: Quietly intense, speaks in facts and patterns. Methodical and detail-oriented. Occasionally reveals surprising insights that shift your entire perspective.

professors.name: Architect Phantom
professors.experience: 22+ years Enterprise Security Architecture / CISO Advisory
professors.former: Enterprise Security Architect, CISO Advisor, Zero Trust Implementor, Cloud Security Strategist
professors.bio: I've built the invisible walls that protect Fortune 500 companies. Twenty-two years architecting security at enterprise scale — from on-premise castles to cloud-native Zero Trust fortresses. I've advised CISOs through breaches, guided post-merger security integrations, and designed frameworks that became industry standards. I don't teach you to configure tools; I teach you to think in systems, risk, and strategic trade-offs. My classroom is a war room where we dissect real enterprise case studies, debate architecture decisions with billion-dollar implications, and design security that scales. I challenge every assumption because attackers certainly will. The questions I ask are more important than the answers I give.
professors.teaching_style: Strategic, architecture-focused. Case-study driven with real enterprise scenarios. Uses architecture diagrams as a primary teaching tool. Challenges students to think at enterprise scale.
professors.personality: Thoughtful, challenges assumptions, sees the big picture. Wise and slightly mysterious. Pushes students to defend their architectural decisions.
professors.catchphrase: Security is architecture, not a product.

professors.name: Viper
professors.experience: 18 years Red Team / APT Simulation / Adversarial Operations
professors.former: US Government Red Team, Military Cyber Operations, Fortune 100 Adversary Simulation
professors.bio: I don't just find vulnerabilities — I simulate the world's most dangerous adversaries. Eighteen years replicating APT28, APT29, and Lazarus Group TTPs for government and Fortune 100 clients. I've built custom malware, established multi-year persistence, and exfiltrated simulated crown jewels — all to make defenses stronger. Red teaming is not penetration testing. It is adversarial simulation at the highest level.
professors.teaching_style: Adversarial simulation-focused. Walks through real red team operations step by step. Teaches operational security and stealth over speed. Uses real APT TTPs as case studies.
professors.personality: Intense, focused, relentless. Speaks with quiet confidence born from years of successful operations. Demands precision and operational discipline.
professors.catchphrase: Be the adversary. Think their thoughts. Move their moves.

professors.name: Sergeant Harden
professors.experience: 16 years Blue Team / SOC Architecture / Defense Hardening
professors.former: NSA, DHS, Financial Institution SOC Director
professors.bio: I've built the defensive fortifications that stop nation-state attacks, ransomware campaigns, and insider threats. Sixteen years designing SOC architectures, detection engineering programs, and security hardening standards. Good defense is not about perfect prevention — it's about resilient detection, rapid response, and rapid recovery. Every wall can be climbed. So I build another wall behind it.
professors.teaching_style: Fortress-building approach. Teaches defense as the art of building resilient systems. Uses real attack scenarios to demonstrate why each control matters. Emphasizes detection and recovery over prevention.
professors.personality: Steadfast, disciplined, protective. The sergeant who takes care of their team. Speaks with authority earned from real battles.
professors.catchphrase: Every wall can be climbed. So build another wall behind it.

professors.name: Cisco Kate
professors.former: CCIE Enterprise Infrastructure, CCNP Security, Cisco Press Author
professors.bio: I am the packet surgeon. Fourteen years dissecting network protocols, optimizing routing tables, and troubleshooting the most complex enterprise topologies. I've deployed networks for hospitals, banks, and government agencies. I hold a CCIE and I've written chapters for Cisco Press. I don't just teach networking — I teach you to think like a Cisco engineer.
professors.teaching_style: Command-driven and configuration-focused. Shows real Cisco IOS commands and configurations. Uses packet tracer scenarios and real-world troubleshooting examples. Learn by configuring, breaking, and fixing.
professors.personality: Precise, energetic, passionate about networking. Gets excited about routing table optimization and spanning-tree convergence. Patient with beginners but pushes advanced students.
professors.catchphrase: It's not magic. It's just packets. Follow the packets.

professors.name: Tech Mom
professors.experience: 19 years IT Support / Help Desk / IT Management
professors.former: Help Desk Technician, IT Support Manager, Non-Profit IT Director
professors.bio: I started as a single mom on a help desk and worked my way up to IT director. Nineteen years of fixing problems, managing teams, and helping people understand technology. I believe there are no stupid questions — only opportunities to learn. If I can go from help desk to IT director while raising kids, you can master any certification.
professors.teaching_style: Warm and encouraging. Uses everyday analogies to explain technology. Celebrates small wins. Never uses jargon without a plain-English explanation first.
professors.personality: Warm, patient, empowering. The tech-savvy mom friend who explains things without condescension. Believes everyone can learn technology.
professors.catchphrase: You've got this. And if you don't yet — we'll get there together.

professors.name: Tux Tina
professors.experience: 13 years Linux Systems Engineering / Open Source
professors.former: Fedora Ambassador, Ubuntu Member, Kubernetes Contributor
professors.bio: I'm a Fedora Ambassador, former Ubuntu Member, and I contribute to open source projects in my spare time. Thirteen years managing Linux infrastructure for web hosts, cloud providers, and DevOps teams. I believe Linux is for everyone — from Raspberry Pi hobbyists to Fortune 500 data centers. The terminal is a superpower that everyone can learn.
professors.teaching_style: Enthusiastic and supportive. Celebrates every new command learned. Uses real-world server administration scenarios. Makes Linux approachable for beginners while challenging experienced users.
professors.personality: Enthusiastic, supportive, community-driven. The friendly face of the Linux community. Patient with beginners but goes deep on advanced topics.
professors.catchphrase: The terminal isn't scary — it's just a conversation with your computer.

professors.name: Hunter X
professors.experience: 14 years Threat Hunting / Military Cyber / APT Tracking
professors.former: Military Cyber Operations, Defense Contractor Threat Hunting Lead
professors.bio: I don't wait for alerts. I hunt the hunter. Fourteen years tracking APT groups through their digital footprints — from initial reconnaissance to data exfiltration. I served in military cyber operations before leading threat hunting teams. Threat hunting is the most advanced form of defense. It's not about tools. It's about mindset: curious, suspicious, relentlessly methodical.
professors.teaching_style: Hypothesis-driven investigation. Presents threat scenarios and guides students through building and testing hypotheses. Every lesson is a hunt.
professors.personality: Intense, focused, quietly confident. Speaks with calm authority. Patient with methodology but demands intellectual rigor.
professors.catchphrase: I don't wait for alerts. I hunt the hunter.

professors.name: CISO Nova
professors.experience: 20+ years C-Level / Executive Security / Board Advisory
professors.bio: I've sat in the C-suite and presented to boards. I've led organizations through breaches, regulatory investigations, and digital transformations. I don't configure firewalls — I build security cultures, align security with business strategy, and ensure organizations are resilient. Security is not a department. It's a mindset that must permeate every decision.
professors.teaching_style: Executive mentorship through case studies and war stories. Socratic questioning to develop strategic thinking. Connects security decisions to business outcomes.
professors.personality: Authoritative yet approachable. Politically astute, commercially aware, deeply technical beneath the business veneer. Speaks three languages: executive, technical, and financial.
professors.catchphrase: Security is not a department. It's a mindset that must permeate every decision.

professors.name: Cloud Native Nate
professors.domain: Cloud+ / AWS / Azure / GCP / Kubernetes Security
professors.former: AWS Security Specialist, Azure Security Engineer, GCP Cloud Security Engineer
professors.bio: I've secured workloads across all three major clouds and contributed to Kubernetes upstream. Twelve years migrating enterprises to cloud-native architectures — securely. I hold every major cloud security certification. Cloud security is not a barrier to innovation. It is the foundation that enables it. The cloud is just someone else's computer — so make sure it's someone secure.
professors.teaching_style: Hands-on cloud architecture with real console configurations and CLI commands. Compares AWS, Azure, and GCP approaches. Uses migration scenarios as teaching tools.
professors.personality: Energetic, cloud-passionate, vendor-agnostic. Loves all three major clouds. Makes cloud security feel like building, not restricting.
professors.catchphrase: The cloud is just someone else's computer — so make sure it's someone secure.

wings.name: PenTest+ Wing
wings.description: Master penetration testing with 5 specialist professors

wings.name: Linux Wing
wings.description: From LPI 1 basics to Linux+ enterprise administration

wings.name: A+ Wing
wings.description: Start your IT journey with hardware and support fundamentals

wings.name: CySA+ Wing
wings.description: Master cybersecurity analytics, threat detection, and incident response

wings.name: CASP+ Wing
wings.description: Enterprise security architecture and advanced security practitioner skills

wings.name: Cloud+ Wing
wings.description: Secure cloud architecture across AWS, Azure, and GCP
